evals.name: obvious-context-labels
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Pick up dry cleaning** — In the One-Off Tasks project, no labels or priority set. Description: 'Suits from the place on High Street, ready after 3pm.'
evals.expected_output: Should silently apply @errands label (if it exists) without asking. Should propose priority and possibly a due date. Should NOT ask about the label — it's obvious from the task.

assertions.name: applies-errands-label
assertions.criteria: The response applies or proposes an @errands context label (or similar location-based label). It does this confidently, not as a question.

assertions.name: proposes-priority
assertions.criteria: The response proposes a priority level for the task.

assertions.name: single-confirmation-round
assertions.criteria: The response presents all proposed changes in one batch and asks for a single confirmation — not multiple rounds of questions.

assertions.name: does-not-rename-task
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose changing the task name or description (unless noting a waiting-for situation).

evals.name: task-with-implied-deadline
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Submit tax return** — In the One-Off Tasks project, no metadata set. Description: 'Self-assessment tax return for 2024/25. The deadline is 31 January 2026.'
evals.expected_output: Should propose a deadline (31 Jan) distinct from a due date (earlier, when to work on it). Should set high priority given it's a legal obligation with a hard deadline.

assertions.name: sets-deadline
assertions.criteria: The response proposes a deadline or hard due date of 31 January 2026 (or equivalent). It recognises this as an external, immovable constraint.

assertions.name: suggests-earlier-due-date
assertions.criteria: The response suggests a due date that is before the deadline — the date to actually work on it, not the final deadline.

assertions.name: sets-high-priority
assertions.criteria: The response proposes a high priority (p1 or p2) given this is a legal obligation with consequences for missing it.

evals.name: someday-maybe-task
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Learn to play guitar** — In a project under the Someday/Maybe area. Description: 'Always wanted to learn, maybe start with online lessons.'
evals.expected_output: Should focus on making the name and description solid for future revisiting. Should skip priority and due date since it's someday/maybe. Context labels still fine if obvious.

assertions.name: skips-priority
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a priority level. It recognises this is a someday/maybe task where priority doesn't apply.

assertions.name: skips-due-date
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a due date or deadline. Someday/maybe items don't get dates.

assertions.name: focuses-on-description-quality
assertions.criteria: The response focuses on ensuring the name and description are clear enough to stand on their own when revisited weeks later.

evals.name: waiting-for-detection
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Book flights to Barcelona** — In the One-Off Tasks project. Description: 'Anniversary trip, first week of June. Need to wait for Sarah to confirm she can get the time off work before booking.'
evals.expected_output: Should detect the waiting-for situation (waiting for Sarah to confirm time off). Should apply @waiting label and note the blocker in the description.

assertions.name: detects-waiting-for
assertions.criteria: The response identifies that this task is blocked — waiting for Sarah to confirm her time off before the user can proceed.

assertions.name: applies-waiting-label
assertions.criteria: The response proposes applying a @waiting label (or similar waiting-for label).

assertions.name: notes-blocker-in-description
assertions.criteria: The response proposes updating the description to note what the task is waiting on (Sarah confirming time off).
